scope: architecture extraction from the gmd-app source-build PoC + one tiny safe in-script refactor; no broad framework, no contract flip, no chart or routing changes

honest_finding.summary: The gmd-app PoC proved that no per-language shell-level branching is needed. Each upstream Dockerfile already encapsulates its own runtime family. Our shell script's one true shared primitive is `build one image from a Dockerfile + context, tag with both registry aliases, push`. Above that, only a small (service -> dockerfile-relpath, context-relpath) map and substrate registry env vars are per-ecosystem.
honest_finding.implication: A `runtime build primitive per family` (build_go, build_node, build_python, build_dotnet, build_java) would be net-negative complexity: it would re-implement what each Dockerfile already does. The minimal honest primitive is one function, not one per family.

primitives.name: build_one_image
primitives.purpose: Build one container image from a Dockerfile and a build context, tag it with both a host push alias and an in-cluster pull alias, optionally push to the host alias.
primitives.where_runtime_specific_logic_actually_li: in the upstream Dockerfile of each service (e.g. repos/gmd-app/src/<service>/Dockerfile). Not in our shell.

rejected_primitives_and_why.rejected: build_go / build_node / build_python / build_dotnet / build_java per-family wrappers
rejected_primitives_and_why.why: Each gmd-app Dockerfile already implements its own multi-stage flow with its own base image and language tooling. Wrapping per-family in shell would duplicate Dockerfile content.
Premature framework. The current explicit (service -> dockerfile, context) map is reviewable and small; introducing skaffold parity now would solve a problem we do not have.

shape.source_root: filesystem path to the ecosystem's app source repo (e.g. repos/gmd-app)

service_context.<service_name>: <source_root>-relative path to the service build context

deliberately_omitted_fields: per-service language/runtime — encapsulated in the Dockerfile
per-service base-image mirroring policy — substrate-level concern
per-service build-args — can be added when a real second-ecosystem need appears
per-service multi-tag promotion policy — release-layer concern, not build-layer

shape.push_registry: where docker push goes (e.g. 'localhost:5000' for local k3d)
shape.pull_registry: image alias used in the tag so in-cluster pulls resolve (e.g. 'host.k3d.internal:5000' for local k3d)

step_1_landed_in_this_slice.what: Extract the inline docker-build-and-push body in build-gmd-app.sh into a shell function `build_one_image(dockerfile_path, context_path, push_image, pull_image, no_push)`. Pure refactor — same observable behavior. Plus an explicit header comment block naming the three layers (shared primitive / per-ecosystem mapping / per-substrate registry).
step_1_landed_in_this_slice.verified_by: Re-running `bash repos/amof/scripts/build-gmd-app.sh --service frontend --tag gmd-dev-c9857ee5` after the refactor: image rebuilt cleanly, pushed to localhost:5000/gmd/frontend:gmd-dev-c9857ee5 with the canonical pull alias baked in. 17/17 command_builder runtime tests still pass.

step_2_when_a_second_ecosystem_appears.what: Copy build-gmd-app.sh to build-<other>-app.sh, swap SERVICE_DOCKERFILE / SERVICE_CONTEXT and the AMOF_<OTHER>_APP_* env defaults. Keep build_one_image identical between the two scripts.
step_2_when_a_second_ecosystem_appears.blocked_until: second ecosystem materialises. Until then, premature.

step_3_when_two_scripts_agree.what: Once two ecosystem build scripts have *identical* `build_one_image` byte-for-byte, hoist it into a shared shell library at `repos/amof/scripts/lib/build_one_image.sh` and `source` it from both. This is the right time to extract because there are two real callers, not a hypothetical one.
step_3_when_two_scripts_agree.blocked_until: step 2 has happened and the two functions provably agree.

step_4_substrate_unification_with_amof_p.what: The sibling `build-cloud-images.sh` already has its own `build_image()` for the AMOF platform substrate. It runs in-cluster (containerd, supports `type=image,name=...,push=true` direct-push without a separate `docker push`). Reconciling the two `build_one_image` shapes would require either: (a) substrate-aware switching inside the function, or (b) keeping two siblings indefinitely. (a) is real framework work and should only be considered after step 3 produces concrete pressure for it.
step_4_substrate_unification_with_amof_p.blocked_until: step 3 lands and a concrete need to share between AMOF platform builds and ecosystem app builds emerges. Until then, keep the two siblings.

tiny_extraction_decision.extraction_done_in_this_slice: Yes — `build_one_image` shell function inside `repos/amof/scripts/build-gmd-app.sh` only. One file changed.
extraction_explicitly_deferred: Cross-script extraction into a shared lib (no second consumer yet).
Any python-level wrapper (command_builder.build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command stays as it is).

commits_landed_to_canonical_main.summary: refactor(gmd-app): extract build_one_image primitive + document layered model

next_slice.rationale: The architecture is now documented and the per-image primitive is extracted. The next leverage point on the path to flipping `gmd` to source_build is the `--all` (or `--all-defaults`) flag on the script + an `all=True` mode on `build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command`. That gives the lifecycle dispatcher a single upstream call that produces the full chart-default image set and unblocks the remaining chart-integration / lifecycle-dispatch / contract-flip slices.
what_to_implement: Add `--all` flag to scripts/build-gmd-app.sh that loops over SERVICE_DOCKERFILE in dispatch order and invokes `build_one_image` for every default service with the same --tag. Honor an `--except <name>` flag to keep shoppingassistantservice opt-in.
Extend `build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command` with an `all=True` mode that emits the new flag instead of `--service <name>`.
Add focused unit tests for the new `--all` argv shape and the deny-list behavior.
Run the host build with `--all` once to verify all 11 images land in the local registry under the same tag.
what_to_explicitly_defer: Cross-script extraction of build_one_image into a shared shell lib (deferred until a second downstream ecosystem exists)
